libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. If generated tests exercise `Compiler`, `SimpleCompiler`, `ExpressionEvaluator`, `ScriptEvaluator`, or `ClassBodyEvaluator` under native image, add the native test build argument `--enable-url-protocols=jrt`. Do not add `commons-compiler` manually: `org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0` already brings `org.codehaus.janino:commons-compiler:3.1.0` transitively. The compile failure shown is from stale test code importing removed internal package `org.codehaus.janino.util.iterator.Iterables`; in 3.1.0 that utility lives under `org.codehaus.commons.compiler.util.iterator.Iterables` in the transitive dependency, or the test can avoid that internal utility.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8855 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Janino has an optional/provided Ant adapter; only tests that intentionally cover `AntCompilerAdapter` would need an Ant test dependency, but normal Janino coverage is reachable without it.

Native image cannot generally define and load arbitrary generated classes at run time, so tests that invoke generated classes may need to distinguish expected native-image dynamic-class-loading limitations from real Janino failures.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Janino does not need an extra Maven dependency or Docker service, but meaningful native-image coverage of its compiler/evaluator APIs needs the JDK `jrt:` URL protocol enabled because Janino 3.1.0 resolves runtime classes such as `java/lang/Object.class` through `ClassLoader.getSystemClassLoader().getResource(...)` on Java 9+.
